note: Frontier LLMs do not expose a vault-as-such — context is a single attention window. The notion 'recall latency as a function of stored-entry count' has no direct frontier analog; see docs/frontier_baselines.md
rationale: CORE's exact-CGA vault is a typed deterministic store with O(N) inner-product scan over Cl(4,1) versors. Frontier systems use attention over a fixed-size context window plus opaque KV-cache; their 'long-context' scaling is dominated by attention's quadratic-in-tokens cost, not a typed-store recall cost. The two architectures are not on the same axis.
